channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump's 'gold card' visa granted to only one person so far, says US commerce chief
item.description: The much-discussed 'gold card' visa, an exclusive U.S. immigration program championed by former President Donald Trump, has been granted to just one individual so far, according to the U.S. Commerce S...

item.title: US Special Forces soldier accused of insider betting using classified intelligence on Maduro operation
item.description: A United States Army Special Forces soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, has been charged with multiple offences after allegedly using classified intelligence to place bets on the anticipated capture of Vene...

item.title: Concerns grow over Reform UK candidate vetting after racist and extremist comments emerge
item.description: A number of Reform UK election candidates have come under scrutiny after past social media posts and online activity revealed racist, inflammatory, and extremist views, raising serious concerns about ...

item.title: Pete Hegseth Dismisses Navy Secretary Amid Clashes Over Shipbuilding and Legal Orders
item.description: Pete Hegseth, the US Defence Secretary, dismissed Navy Secretary John Phelan after a series of clashes, including disagreements over shipbuilding plans and the refusal to disregard a judge’s ruling. T...

item.title: US soldier charged for using classified information to profit from bets on Venezuela operation
item.description: A US Army soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, has been charged for allegedly using classified military information to make substantial profits through online prediction markets. According to the US Departme...

item.title: Twin Cities Areas Stay Closed Amid US-Iran Talks Uncertainty
item.description: Uncertainty lingers over expected US-Iran peace talks, keeping parts of Rawalpindi and Islamabad locked down for five days straight. Areas around Nur Khan Airbase, including Shah Faisal Colony, Khalid...

item.title: World Cup Final Tickets Available for Purchase at Record Price
item.description: Tickets for the upcoming World Cup final are now available for sale, but their price tag has drawn significant attention. The cost for a single ticket has been set at an eye-watering £23 million, a pr...

item.title: Man seriously injured following stabbing outside Scarborough apartment building
item.description: A man was left with serious injuries after a stabbing incident outside an apartment building in Scarborough, Toronto, on April 23, 2026. Emergency crews responded to the scene following reports of a v...

item.title: Russian Military Scholars Publish Paper Accusing Britain of Supporting Ukrainian Sabotage and Retaining Colonial Artefacts
item.description: Two Russian scholars from the Academy of Military Sciences have released an academic paper in the journal Representative Power – XXI Century, sharply criticising the British establishment. The authors...

item.title: Trump extends cease-fire with Iran while keeping ports under naval blockade
item.description: The Wall Street Journal’s live coverage of the Iran war on April 21, 2026, reports that President Donald Trump has decided to extend the current cease‑fire with Iran while maintaining a U.S. naval blo...

item.title: Jealousy Suspected in Murder of Tanzanian Nurse in Kilimanjaro
item.description: In Kilimanjaro Region, Tanzania, a tragic incident occurred on April 20, 2026, where 25-year-old nursing officer Asha Bakari was allegedly killed by her ex-boyfriend, a boda boda rider. The suspect re...

item.title: Vihiga police detain father after 9-week-old baby goes missing under unclear circumstances
item.description: There is serious tension and worry in Mbale, Vihiga County, after a nine-week-old baby girl disappeared in a very troubling situation involving her father. Reports indicate that the man left home carr...

item.title: Saudi Arabia Opens 2026 Scholarship Applications for Kenyan and International Students
item.description: Saudi Arabia has officially opened applications for its 2026 scholarship programme targeting Kenyan and other international students who wish to pursue higher education in the Kingdom. The initiative,...

item.title: Pakistanis Selected for China’s Manned Space Programme
item.description: This month brings a source of national pride for Pakistanis as two of their citizens, Zeeshan Ali and Khurram Daud, have been selected for China’s manned space programme after successfully passing sev...

item.title: Canada’s U.S. ambassador says trade tensions are serious but ties must be preserved
item.description: Canada’s new ambassador to the United States, Mark Wiseman, told the House of Commons foreign affairs committee that relations between Canada and the U.S. are “too important” to abandon even as trade ...

item.title: Tom Brady Shares Guidance for NFL Rookies and Welcomes No. 1 Pick Fernando Mendoza to the Raiders
item.description: Tom Brady, a seven-time Super Bowl champion and minority owner of the Las Vegas Raiders, shared a message of encouragement and realism for incoming NFL rookies during the 2026 NFL Draft. As dozens of ...

item.title: Nine-year-old South African chess prodigy raises funds to compete internationally
item.description: Nine-year-old Atlehang Malemane, a rising chess talent from South Africa, has successfully raised over R105,000 via a BackaBuddy crowdfunding campaign to participate in the Commonwealth Chess Champion...

item.title: Giants Strengthen Roster with Edge Rusher Arvell Reese and Offensive Lineman Francis Mauigoa in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: During the 2026 NFL Draft, the New York Giants focused on reinforcing their roster in critical positions, selecting two highly rated college players. With the fifth overall pick, the Giants acquired O...

item.title: Urban Farming Thrives Amid the Busy Streets of Bonifacio Global City
item.description: Urban farming has begun to take root right in the bustling center of Bonifacio Global City (BGC), showcasing a sustainable approach to city living. The initiative, known locally as 'Tao Po,' integrate...

item.title: Ladakh launches village reservoirs under Project Him Sarovar to store glacier and snowmelt for year‑round use
item.description: Ladakh has started building a network of village reservoirs under a new initiative called Project Him Sarovar, aimed at tackling growing water stress in the Union Territory’s high‑altitude cold desert...
